title: After lawsuit, Secretary of Commonwealth agrees to produce legally mandated report on voting in jail
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Secretary William Galvin agrees to complete mandated voting reports after lawsuit, citing previous delays as 'technical issues.'
The agreement follows the VOTES Act, requiring reports post-election on incarcerated voters' access to voting.
The secretary's office has six months to deliver overdue reports with data improvements before the Sept. 1 primary.

title: New Patriots fullback Reggie Gilliam sees a similarity between Drake Maye and former MVP
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Reggie Gilliam signed a three-year contract with the New England Patriots after six years with the Buffalo Bills.
Gilliam compares new Patriots quarterback Drake Maye to former MVP Josh Allen in terms of physical build and potential.
Gilliam aims to boost the Patriots' running game, helping Rhamondre Stevenson and TreVeyon Henderson as a physical fullback.

title: Red Sox infielder Romy Gonzalez undergoes shoulder surgery; Boston signs lefty reliever ​
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox infielder Romy Gonzalez had shoulder surgery and will miss at least the first two months of 2026.
Gonzalez was crucial in 2025, batting .305 with nine homers, primarily performing well against left-handed pitching.
Red Sox signed veteran lefty Danny Coulombe to a one-year deal to strengthen their bullpen before the 2026 season.

title: High school student allegedly killed Danvers woman in her home in ‘random’ attack, DA says
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: A Bishop Fenwick High School senior is charged with murder after Janet Swallow, 68, was found dead in her Danvers home.
Anthony DeMayo, 18, was reportedly found with a blood-stained knife in Lynn and later linked to the Danvers murder.
Authorities described the killing as a random act and found no connection between DeMayo and the victim, Janet Swallow.

title: Boston police seeking suspect who allegedly assaulted pregnant woman in Roxbury laundromat
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston police seek suspect who allegedly assaulted a pregnant woman at a Roxbury laundromat on March 1.
The suspect allegedly kicked the victim after a payment card dispute at 104 Warren St. and fled to Moreland St.
Police are searching for a 6’0” light-skinned male and a woman who may be linked to the incident.

title: Fired Mass. cop arrested in N.H. for allegedly impersonating a police officer
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Mikel Beltran, 37, was arrested in Londonderry, N.H. for allegedly impersonating a police officer using blue lights.
Beltran, a former Lawrence officer, was terminated in March 2025 after incidents involving a handgun and inappropriate behavior.
Beltran is suing the Lawrence police, alleging unconstitutional phone searches led to his firing, per court documents.

title: NFL insider believes Eagles will lower asking price to Patriots in A.J. Brown trade
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Patriots are reportedly pursuing a trade for Eagles wide receiver A.J. Brown.
NFL insider suggests the Eagles may lower their trade demand for A.J. Brown after June 1.
Patriots executive Eliot Wolf stated the team is exploring all offseason options, including acquiring Brown.

title: Truck catches fire on Mass. Pike during early-morning commute, causing lane shutdowns
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A truck caught fire on the Massachusetts Turnpike in Westborough around 8 a.m., closing westbound lanes temporarily.
Firefighters from Westborough and Grafton extinguished the blaze by 8:30 a.m.; no injuries were reported.
The westbound lanes reopened by 1:30 p.m. after an investigation into the fire's cause commenced.

title: New Patriots WR Romeo Doubs explains why he chose New England in free agency
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Romeo Doubs signed a four-year, $80 million contract with the New England Patriots in free agency.
Doubs cited quarterback Drake Maye and the Patriots' winning culture as key reasons for joining the team.
Doubs previously led the Green Bay Packers in 2025 with 55 catches, 724 yards, and six touchdowns.

title: SUV owner experiences a serious – and not uncommon – engine issue
author: John Paul
bullets: A 2018 Hyundai Tucson owner experienced engine failure, requiring replacement under warranty due to manufacturing defects.
Hyundai/Kia models commonly suffer from engine issues caused by improperly machined bearings leading to power loss.
Toyota and Chevrolet are also recalling vehicles for engine failures due to similar manufacturing issues.
